item.title: Meta Hires Former Google, Stripe Executives Behind AI Startup Dreamer
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. has hired the founders and team behind the artificial intelligence startup Dreamer, which launched earlier this year to help people create their own AI agents.

item.title: US to Commit $250 Million Toward Supply Chain Investment Fund
item.description: The US plans to contribute $250 million toward an investment consortium that would help fund projects aimed at strengthening supply chains for energy and critical minerals, according to a Trump administration official.

item.title: JPMorgan Offers Clients a New Way to Hedge AI Debt Risk
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is offering clients a new way to bet against the debt of five hyperscalers, as investors seek more-liquid hedges amid an unprecedented borrowing spree to finance artificial-intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Sheinbaum Allies Rush to Lure Private Infrastructure Investment as Economy Sputters
item.description: President Claudia Sheinbaum’s allies in congress are in a hurry to lure more private investment by speeding up project approvals, clear-eyed that public spending alone isn’t sufficient to boost lackluster growth in Latin America’s second-biggest economy.

item.title: Apple to Start AI Comeback Bid on June 8 With Annual WWDC Conference
item.description: Apple Inc. announced plans to hold its annual Worldwide Developers Conference from June 8 to June 12, when the company is poised to unveil a make-or-break collection of AI features.

item.title: Bank of Canada Recruits New Officials as Kozicki, Mendes to Depart
item.description: The Bank of Canada is looking at internal candidates to fill spots on its rate-setting governing council after two deputy governors said they’ll leave the organization in the coming months.

item.title: Sheryl Sandberg Says Nscale and Its CEO Remind Her of Early Facebook
item.description: Sheryl Sandberg is back at a technology startup — and it reminds her of the halcyon days at Google and Facebook.

item.title: Israel Says Economy Lost Over $57 Billion During Two Years of War in Gaza
item.description: Israel lost 8.6% of annual gross domestic product, or 177 billion shekels ($57 billion), during the two years through 2025, figures that demonstrate the economic toll of the country’s state of near constant conflict.

item.title: HPE Assets Struggled With Buyer Interest in Antitrust Divestment
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. struggled to interest some investors and other tech companies in buying assets that the US government required be sold to clear its takeover of Juniper Networks, receiving bids that ranged between $1 and $15 million, according to court records.

item.title: Kalshi, Polymarket Founders Back New Prediction Market VC Fund
item.description: A new venture capital fund focused on prediction markets is launching with initial investments from a long list of prominent investors including two of the industry’s biggest players, Polymarket’s Shayne Coplan and Kalshi Inc.’s Tarek Mansour, according to an investment document viewed by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Citadel Securities’ Rubner Sees Short Squeeze Fueling US Stocks Rally
item.description: A record streak of short bets is at risk of unwinding, priming US equities for a comeback with hedge funds and systematic strategies poised to drive the next wave of buying, according to Citadel Securities’ Scott Rubner.

item.title: Apple Is Set to Put Ads in Apple Maps in Services Push
item.description: Apple Inc. is preparing to introduce advertising in its Maps app, part of a broader push to generate more money from services.

item.title: SpaceX Holder Fidelity Sees Path for Musk’s Data-in-Orbit Vision
item.description: Elon Musk’s bold vision for putting AI data centers in space, tapping into solar power and bypassing terrestrial worries such as electric grids and neighbors’ opposition, hinges on SpaceX’s ability to perfect its Starship booster.

item.title: Namibia Turns Down Starlink’s Bid to Operate in the Nation
item.description: Namibia turned down a bid by billionaire Elon Musk’s Starlink service to operate in the country, denying the satellite internet provider both a telecommunications service license and access to radio spectrum.

item.title: Supertanker Hauling Iraqi Oil Crosses Hormuz With Its Signal Off
item.description: An oil supertanker hauling two-million barrels of Iraq’s crude got through the Strait of Hormuz, the first vessel observed moving Baghdad’s barrels through the the vital waterway since it all but closed to commercial shipping because of the Iran war.

item.title: ECB’s Lane Says AI Is Another Reason to Finish EU Savings Union
item.description: Europe’s reliance on bank-based funding prevents the continent from reaping the full benefits of innovation centering on artificial intelligence, according to European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane.

item.title: Samsung Phones Can Now Share With iPhones Using Apple’s AirDrop Protocol
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co.’s latest smartphones can share photos, videos and other files with iPhone owners using Apple Inc.’s AirDrop, marking a new level of compatibility between the industry rivals that would have been unthinkable a few years ago.

item.title: German Two-Year Bond Futures Trading Halted Amid Volatility
item.description: Trading in two-year German bond futures was halted twice on Monday, leaving traders struggling to keep up with swings triggered by US President Donald Trump’s decision to back down from strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure.

item.title: High-Grade Borrowers Jump Back Into US Bond Market After Halt
item.description: The US investment-grade bond market is reopening Monday following a three-session pause in activity, as concerns over the Iran conflict ease.

item.title: TotalEnergies Released From $1 Billion US Offshore Wind Lease
item.description: The Trump administration released TotalEnergies SE from $1 billion in offshore wind leases so the French energy giant can redirect those funds into oil and natural gas investments in the US.

item.title: The Chokepoint to End All Chokepoints
item.description: What matters is relative flows.

item.title: David Simon, Shopping Mall King, Dies at 64 After Cancer Fight
item.description: David Simon, the chairman and chief executive officer of US shopping mall heavyweight Simon Property Group, died of cancer. He was 64.

item.title: UK Studies Wider Ways to Help Economy on Iran, Starmer Says
item.description: Keir Starmer said he is looking at wider measures to support Britain’s economy through the Iran crisis as he prepares to chair an emergency meeting on the economic impact of the conflict.

item.title: Faraday Future Says SEC Not Recommending Enforcement Action
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission is not recommending an enforcement action against electric vehicle startup Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. after a years-long investigation, according to a company filing.

item.title: JPMorgan Kicks Off $8 Billion Junk-Bond Sale for EA Buyout
item.description: Wall Street banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. have kicked off the sale of $8 billion in junk bonds to fund the record leveraged buyout of video game maker Electronic Arts Inc.

item.title: Polymarket Implements New Insider Trading Rules After Scrutiny
item.description: Polymarket has moved to squash some types of insider trading after the prediction markets platform came under scrutiny for suspected manipulation.

item.title: Trump Backs Off Iran Energy Strike Threat as Markets Reel
item.description: Just before US markets opened on Monday, President Donald Trump backed down from his threat to destroy Iran’s power infrastructure if it didn’t reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours — pushed by the surge in energy prices and global market rout unleashed by the US-Israeli war on Tehran and hopes that fresh talks could yield a deal to end the conflict.

item.title: Blackstone Weighs First-Ever Sports Investment With Cricket Bet
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is considering whether to buy a stake in the world’s most lucrative professional cricket league through its fund for wealthy individuals, in what would be the first sports investment for the world’s biggest alternative asset manager.

item.title: Saylor’s Strategy Returns to Common Stock for Latest Bitcoin Buy
item.description: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. purchased roughly $76.5 million worth of Bitcoin, turning back to its common stock to fund the latest acquisition following a week of relying on its preferred shares.

item.title: Isar Aerospace in Talks to Raise €250 Million Ahead of Launch
item.description: German rocket startup Isar Aerospace is in talks to raise €250 million ($289 million) in funding ahead of a key launch attempt planned for this week, according to a person familiar with the matter.
